You are a programming expert. 
You will be provided with a patch used to fix a bug. 

Your task is summarize the modifications made in the patch.
Your summary should consist of two parts, modifications in each file and their relationships.

From eccc31ebce5430f9e91b9684c63eaaf885e603f9 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: Lukasz Lenart <lukaszlenart@apache.org>
Date: Tue, 14 Jun 2016 06:53:36 +0200
Subject: [PATCH] Improves validation RegEx


diff --git a/xwork-core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java b/xwork-core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java
index 767416deb..9aa3c9d44 100644
--- a/xwork-core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java
+++ b/xwork-core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java
@@ -82,7 +82,7 @@ public class URLValidator extends FieldValidatorSupport {
                     "|((\\d|[1-9]\\d|1\\d{2}|2[0-4][0-9]|25[0-5])\\.){3}" +
                     "(\\d|[1-9]\\d|1\\d{2}|2[0-4][0-9]|25[0-5])" +
                     ")(:\\d+)?" +
-                    ")(((\\/+([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*" +
+                    ")(((\\/{0,1}([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*" +
                     "(\\?([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)" +
                     "?)?)?" +
                     "(#([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)?" +

diff --git a/xwork-core/src/test/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/URLValidatorTest.java b/xwork-core/src/test/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/URLValidatorTest.java
index f49555734..da9806a96 100644
--- a/xwork-core/src/test/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/URLValidatorTest.java
+++ b/xwork-core/src/test/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/URLValidatorTest.java
@@ -139,6 +139,7 @@ public class URLValidatorTest extends XWorkTestCase {
         assertFalse(pattern.matcher("").matches());
         assertFalse(pattern.matcher("   ").matches());
         assertFalse(pattern.matcher("no url").matches());
+		assertFalse(pattern.matcher("http://example.com////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////??").matches());
 
         assertTrue(pattern.matcher("http://www.opensymphony.com").matches());
         assertTrue(pattern.matcher("https://www.opensymphony.com").matches());
-- 
2.34.1

### Modifications in Each File

1. **URLValidator.java**
   - The regular expression used for URL validation has been modified. Specifically, the part of the regex that matches the path component of the URL has been changed from:
     ```
     "(((\\/+([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*"
     ```
     to:
     ```
     "(((\\/{0,1}([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*"
     ```
   - This change allows for an optional leading slash in the path component of the URL.

2. **URLValidatorTest.java**
   - A new test case has been added to validate the URL regex. The test checks that a URL with excessive slashes and question marks, specifically `"http://example.com////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////??"`, does not match the regex, ensuring that the updated regex correctly handles such cases.

### Relationships Between Modifications

The modification in `URLValidator.java` enhances the URL validation logic by allowing for a more flexible path component in the regex. The corresponding change in `URLValidatorTest.java` introduces a test case that specifically checks for edge cases related to the new regex pattern. This relationship ensures that the new regex is not only implemented but also validated against potential misuse or edge cases, thereby improving the robustness of the URL validation functionality.

2. output the file name that is most likely to cause the bug.

From eccc31ebce5430f9e91b9684c63eaaf885e603f9 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: Lukasz Lenart <lukaszlenart@apache.org>
Date: Tue, 14 Jun 2016 06:53:36 +0200
Subject: [PATCH] Improves validation RegEx


diff --git a/xwork-core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java b/xwork-core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java
index 767416deb..9aa3c9d44 100644
--- a/xwork-core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java
+++ b/xwork-core/src/main/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/validators/URLValidator.java
@@ -82,7 +82,7 @@ public class URLValidator extends FieldValidatorSupport {
                     "|((\\d|[1-9]\\d|1\\d{2}|2[0-4][0-9]|25[0-5])\\.){3}" +
                     "(\\d|[1-9]\\d|1\\d{2}|2[0-4][0-9]|25[0-5])" +
                     ")(:\\d+)?" +
-                    ")(((\\/+([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*" +
+                    ")(((\\/{0,1}([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*" +
                     "(\\?([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)" +
                     "?)?)?" +
                     "(#([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)?" +

diff --git a/xwork-core/src/test/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/URLValidatorTest.java b/xwork-core/src/test/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/URLValidatorTest.java
index f49555734..da9806a96 100644
--- a/xwork-core/src/test/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/URLValidatorTest.java
+++ b/xwork-core/src/test/java/com/opensymphony/xwork2/validator/URLValidatorTest.java
@@ -139,6 +139,7 @@ public class URLValidatorTest extends XWorkTestCase {
         assertFalse(pattern.matcher("").matches());
         assertFalse(pattern.matcher("   ").matches());
         assertFalse(pattern.matcher("no url").matches());
+		assertFalse(pattern.matcher("http://example.com////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////??").matches());
 
         assertTrue(pattern.matcher("http://www.opensymphony.com").matches());
         assertTrue(pattern.matcher("https://www.opensymphony.com").matches());
-- 
2.34.1

### Modifications in Each File

1. **URLValidator.java**
   - The regular expression used for URL validation has been modified. Specifically, the part of the regex that matches the path component of the URL has been changed from:
     ```
     "(((\\/+([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*"
     ```
     to:
     ```
     "(((\\/{0,1}([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*"
     ```
   - This change allows for an optional leading slash in the path component of the URL.

2. **URLValidatorTest.java**
   - A new test case has been added to check the behavior of the URL validator with a specific input: 
     ```
     "http://example.com////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////??"
     ```
   - This test asserts that the input does not match the URL pattern, ensuring that excessively long paths with multiple slashes and query parameters are correctly identified as invalid.

### Relationships Between Modifications

The modification in `URLValidator.java` enhances the URL validation logic by allowing for an optional leading slash in the path component of the URL. This change is directly related to the new test case added in `URLValidatorTest.java`, which tests the validator's response to a URL with an excessive number of slashes. The test ensures that the updated regex behaves as expected and correctly identifies invalid URLs, thereby validating the effectiveness of the changes made to the URL validation logic.

### Modifications in Each File

1. **URLValidator.java**
   - The regular expression used for URL validation has been modified. Specifically, the part of the regex that matches the path component of the URL has been changed from:
     ```
     "(((\\/+([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*"
     ```

     to:

   - This change allows for an optional leading slash in the path component of the URL.

2. **URLValidatorTest.java**
   - A new test case has been added to validate the URL regex. The test checks that a URL with excessive slashes and question marks, specifically `"http://example.com////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////??"`, does not match the regex, ensuring that the updated regex correctly handles such cases.

The modification in `URLValidator.java` enhances the URL validation logic by allowing for an optional leading slash in the path component of the URL. This change is directly related to the new test case added in `URLValidatorTest.java`, which aims to ensure that the updated regex behaves as expected, particularly in rejecting malformed URLs with excessive slashes and question marks. The test serves to validate the effectiveness of the regex modification, ensuring that the URL validation logic is robust and accurate.

2. explain how the bug is fixed, including code statements fixing the bug and why.

Here is the commit message:
Improves validation RegEx

Here is the root cause:
The original regular expression in `URLValidator.java` did not account for an optional leading slash in the path component of the URL, which could lead to incorrect validation results for certain valid URLs. This oversight allowed malformed URLs with excessive slashes to be incorrectly accepted as valid.

Here is the patch:
      */
     public String getUrlRegex() {
         if (StringUtils.isNotEmpty(urlRegexExpression)) {
             return (String) parse(urlRegexExpression, String.class);
         } else if (StringUtils.isNotEmpty(urlRegex)) {
             return urlRegex;
         } else {
             return "^(https?|ftp):\\/\\/" +
                     "(([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;\\?&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})+" +
                     "(:([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;\\?&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})+)?" +
                     "@)?(#?" +
                     ")((([a-z0-9]\\.|[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]\\.)*" +
                     "[a-z][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]" +
                     "|((\\d|[1-9]\\d|1\\d{2}|2[0-4][0-9]|25[0-5])\\.){3}" +
                     "(\\d|[1-9]\\d|1\\d{2}|2[0-4][0-9]|25[0-5])" +
                     ")(:\\d+)?" +
-                    ")(((\\/+([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*" +
+                    ")(((\\/{0,1}([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*" +
                     "(\\?([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)" +
                     "?)?)?" +
                     "(#([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)?" +
                     "$";
         }
     }
 


Provide a response strictly in the following format:

Reason:
<text>

The code statements that lead to the bug are:
```java
"(((\\/+([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*"
```
Reason:
The original regular expression incorrectly required one or more leading slashes in the path component of the URL, which did not account for valid URLs that may have an optional leading slash. This oversight allowed malformed URLs with excessive slashes to be incorrectly accepted as valid.

The code statements that fix the bug are:
```java
"(((\\/{0,1}([a-z0-9$_\\.\\+!\\*\\'\\(\\),;:@&=-]|%[0-9a-f]{2})*)*"
```
Reason:
The updated regular expression allows for zero or one leading slash in the path component of the URL, thus correctly validating URLs that may or may not start with a slash, preventing the acceptance of malformed URLs.
